column1: #   two-column lines (no tag) default to [stdout] for backwards compat
# first match wins. specific before general.
# edit this file to teach your machine new intents.
# languages: add a section. translate the intents, keep the commands.
# desktop/downloads: remove these if your system doesn't have them
# These answer "what system call does X" without hitting T2

column2: echo "fork() - creates child process by duplicating the calling process"
echo "fork() - creates child process by duplicating the calling process"
echo "fork() - creates child process by duplicating the calling process"
echo "fork() - system call that creates a child process by duplicating the calling process"
echo "pipe() - creates a unidirectional data channel for IPC"
echo "pipe() - creates a unidirectional data channel for IPC"
echo "pipe() - system call creating unidirectional data channel between processes"
echo "exec() family (execl, execv, execve, execvp) - replaces current process image"
echo "exec() family (execl, execv, execve, execvp) - replaces current process image"
echo "exec() family - replaces current process image with a new program"
echo "wait() or waitpid() - suspends execution until a child process terminates"
echo "wait() or waitpid() - suspends execution until a child process terminates"
echo "wait()/waitpid() - blocks parent until child process terminates"
echo "mmap() - maps files or devices into memory"
echo "mmap() - maps files or devices into memory"
echo "mmap() - maps files or devices into memory for efficient I/O"
echo "open() - opens a file and returns a file descriptor"
echo "open() - system call that opens a file and returns a file descriptor"
echo "close() - system call that releases a file descriptor"
echo "read() - reads data from a file descriptor into a buffer"
echo "read() - reads bytes from file descriptor into buffer"
echo "write() - writes data from a buffer to a file descriptor"
echo "write() - writes bytes from buffer to file descriptor"
echo "dup()/dup2() - duplicates file descriptor, used for I/O redirection"
echo "socket() - creates an endpoint for communication"
echo "bind() - assigns an address to a socket"
echo "listen() - marks socket as passive, accepting incoming connections"
echo "accept() - extracts first connection request from queue"
echo "connect() - initiates connection on a socket"
echo "select() - monitors multiple file descriptors for I/O readiness"
echo "poll() - waits for events on file descriptors"
echo "epoll - Linux scalable I/O event notification (epoll_create, epoll_ctl, epoll_wait)"
echo "signal()/sigaction() - sets disposition of a signal"
echo "kill() - sends signal to a process"
echo "kill() - system call that sends a signal to a process or process group"
echo "stat() - retrieves file metadata (size, permissions, timestamps)"
echo "unlink() - removes a file (deletes directory entry)"
echo "chown() - changes file owner and group"
echo "gzip, bzip2, xz for single files; tar -czvf for directories; zip -r for cross-platform"
echo "Type what you want. subtract translates to the command."
echo "grep - search for patterns in files"
echo "find - search for files in directory hierarchy"
echo "chown - change file owner and group"
echo "kill - send signal to process (default: SIGTERM)"
echo "awk - pattern scanning and text processing"
echo "sed - stream editor for text transformation"
echo "xargs - build and execute commands from stdin"
echo "tee - read stdin, write to stdout and files"
echo "diff - compare files line by line"
echo "patch - apply diff output to files"
echo "apropos - search manual page names and descriptions"
echo "PATH - colon-separated list of directories the shell searches for commands"
echo "HOME - environment variable pointing to your home directory (~)"
echo "export VAR=value (current shell) or add to ~/.bashrc for persistence"
echo "Ctrl-C cancels the running command. Ctrl-Z suspends it (resume with fg)."
echo "Ctrl-C cancels the running command. Ctrl-Z suspends it (resume with fg)."
echo "Ctrl-C cancels the running command. Ctrl-Z suspends it (resume with fg)."
echo "| sends output of one command as input to another. Example: ls | grep txt"
echo "| sends output of one command as input to another. Example: ls | grep txt"
echo "> writes output to file (overwrites). >> appends. < reads file as input."
echo "> redirects output to a file, overwriting it. >> appends instead."
echo "stdin (fd 0) - standard input. Where programs read input from (usually keyboard)."
echo "stdout (fd 1) - standard output. Where programs write normal output (usually terminal)."
echo "stderr (fd 2) - standard error. Where programs write error messages, separate from stdout."
echo "An integer the kernel uses to track open files. 0=stdin, 1=stdout, 2=stderr"
echo "/dev/null - data sink; redirecting here discards output: cmd > /dev/null"
echo "symlink - pointer to another file; ln -s target linkname"
echo "symlink - pointer to another file; ln -s target linkname"
echo "sudo - execute a command as superuser (root). Required for system-level changes."
echo "root - the superuser account (UID 0) with full system access"
echo "man <command> for manual, <command> --help for quick usage, apropos <keyword> to search"
